rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks at the correct scope level
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Place assignments in implementation scope only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable
generic_rules: Maintain consistent scope hierarchy when inserting annex blocks
Ensure inserted annex blocks do not introduce new dependencies on implementation details
Preserve existing component type contracts when adding implementation-level bindings
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators for generated contracts
anti_patterns: Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
